Web search results for query: "list grid view toggle UX pattern density switcher 2024 design system compact comfortable cozy"

Links: [{"title":"Grid / List Toggle Switch – CodeMyUI","url":"https://codemyui.com/grid-list-toggle-switch/"},{"title":"Carbon Design System","url":"https://carbondesignsystem.com/components/content-switcher/usage/"},{"title":"How to design a toggle list?","url":"https://cieden.com/book/atoms/toggle-switch/how-to-design-a-toggle-list"},{"title":"Toggle button design: the full run through - Justinmind","url":"https://www.justinmind.com/ui-design/toggle-button-patterns-examples"},{"title":"Building a List/Grid View Switcher with jQuery","url":"https://www.vandelaydesign.com/list-grid-view-switch/"},{"title":"Grid / List view toggle by Aaron Iker on Dribbble","url":"https://dribbble.com/shots/10774461-Grid-List-view-toggle"},{"title":"Data Table Pattern | UX Patterns for Developers","url":"https://uxpatterns.dev/patterns/data-display/table"},{"title":"Table switching between list view and grid view by Helena Lozano for OutSystems on Dribbble","url":"https://dribbble.com/shots/20290994-Table-switching-between-list-view-and-grid-view"}]

Here are the search results for "list grid view toggle UX pattern density switcher 2024 design system":

**Key Findings:**

**Carbon Design System Approach:**
A content switcher can be used for binary views, like switching between a grid and list view. There are three height sizes for the content switcher—small (32px), medium (40px), and large (48px)—with the choice depending on your layout's density or the switcher's prominence.

**Common Use Cases:**
Content switchers are frequently used to let users toggle between different formats, like a grid view and a table view, and are also often used to narrow large content groups or to sort related content.

**Real-World Example:**
Booking.com's toggle design uses a simple and clear layout with text labels "List" and "Grid" that directly indicate the function of each option.

**Best Practices:**
A toggle button should have an immediate effect and never depend on any "submit" or "save" buttons, acting as a stand-alone control.

The search results focus primarily on list/grid view toggles as a binary view switcher pattern, though they don't specifically address "density switcher" variations like compact, comfortable, or cozy states within a single design system.
